question.text: As per SEBI circular dated 10/05/2023, FPIs are allowed to have which type of access for participating in Exchange Traded Commodity Derivatives?

answer.text: Direct Market Access (DMA) using broker terminal and infrastructure
Access only through domestic institutional investors
Proprietary trading access without broker intermediation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the SEBI circular dated 10/05/2023, FPIs are allowed to have Direct Market Access (DMA) using broker terminal and broker infrastructure for ETCDs. **The Trap:** FPIs are specifically permitted DMA through broker infrastructure, not exclusively through custodians or domestic institutions. They don't get proprietary access. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers FPI participation framework in equity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** DMA for FPIs in ETCDs reduces latency and improves execution quality — this regulatory change makes Indian commodity markets more attractive to foreign institutional participants.
